docs.title: AxiomGuard
docs.text:
AxiomGuard is not smart. It is correct.

Deterministic verification engine for LLM outputs. Powered by Z3 Theorem Prover. Fully auditable. Zero false positives. You write the rules. We enforce them with mathematical proof.

docs.title: What is AxiomGuard?
docs.text:
AxiomGuard is a Hybrid Neuro-Symbolic verification layer that uses formal mathematics (Z3 SMT Solver) to mathematically prove whether LLM responses contain hallucinations.

from axiomguard import verify

result = verify("The company is in Chiang Mai", ["The company is in Bangkok"])
print(result.is_hallucinating)  # True — Z3 proved it mathematically

Not a guess — a proof

When AxiomGuard says it's a hallucination, it's backed by a formal mathematical proof from the Z3 theorem prover. Zero false positives on proven contradictions.

docs.title: Why AxiomGuard?
docs.text:
Standard RAG pipelines retrieve context using vector similarity — but vectors encode similarity, not truth:

"The company is in Bangkok"     → vector A
"The company is in Chiang Mai"  → vector B

cosine_similarity(A, B) = 0.96  ← Almost identical — but contradictory!
Feature AxiomGuard Prompt-based Embedding filters Deterministic (zero false positives) Yes No No Explainable (proof trace) Yes No No Self-correcting Yes No No Zero token cost for verification Yes No Yes Latency ~10ms 500ms+ ~10ms

docs.title: Two Ways to Create Rules (BYOR)
docs.text:
AxiomGuard is an enforcement engine. You write the rules — we enforce them.

YAML (Recommended)Programmatic (RuleBuilder) 
rules:
  - name: hq_location
    type: unique
    entity: company
    relation: location
    value: Bangkok
    severity: error
    message: "HQ is Bangkok — not negotiable."
    #         ↑ This EXACT string is returned on violation.

from axiomguard import RuleBuilder

kb = (
    RuleBuilder(domain="company")
    .unique("hq", entity="company", relation="location",
            value="Bangkok", message="HQ is Bangkok.")
    .to_knowledge_base()
)

docs.title: verify(response, axioms)
docs.text:
Verify an LLM response against ground-truth axiom strings.

from axiomguard import verify

result = verify(
    response="The CEO is John",
    axioms=["The CEO is Somchai"],
)
result.is_hallucinating  # True
result.reason            # "Z3 proved contradiction (UNSAT): ..."

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description response str The LLM response to verify axioms list[str] Ground-truth statements

Returns: VerificationResult

docs.title: verify_with_kb(response, kb)
docs.text:
Verify against a KnowledgeBase with compiled YAML rules.

from axiomguard import KnowledgeBase, verify_with_kb

kb = KnowledgeBase()
kb.load("rules.axiom.yml")
result = verify_with_kb("The CEO is John", kb)

docs.title: verify_chunks(chunks, kb)
docs.text:
Verify and annotate a list of RAG chunks in bulk.

from axiomguard import verify_chunks

annotated = verify_chunks(chunks, kb)

docs.title: generate_with_guard(prompt, kb, llm_generate, ...)
docs.text:
Self-correcting generation loop.

from axiomguard import generate_with_guard

result = generate_with_guard(
    prompt="Tell me about the company",
    kb=kb,
    llm_generate=my_llm,
    max_retries=2,
)
result.status    # "verified" | "corrected" | "failed"
result.response  # Final response text
result.attempts  # Number of attempts

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description prompt str User prompt kb KnowledgeBase Rules to verify against llm_generate Callable[[str], str] LLM generation function max_retries int Maximum correction attempts (default: 2)

Returns: CorrectionResult

docs.title: extract_claims(text)
docs.text:
Extract SRO triples from natural language text.

from axiomguard import extract_claims

claims = extract_claims("The company is in Bangkok and the CEO is Somchai")
# [Claim(subject="company", relation="location", object="Bangkok"),
#  Claim(subject="company", relation="ceo", object="Somchai")]

docs.title: translate_to_logic(text)
docs.text:
Translate a single sentence to an SRO triple dict.

from axiomguard import translate_to_logic

triple = translate_to_logic("The company is in Bangkok")
# {"subject": "company", "relation": "location", "object": "Bangkok"}

docs.title: RuleBuilder
docs.text:
Fluent API for building rules programmatically.

from axiomguard import RuleBuilder

kb = (
    RuleBuilder(domain="test")
    .entity("company", aliases=["firm"])
    .unique("hq", entity="company", relation="location", value="Bangkok")
    .range_rule("salary", entity="employee", relation="salary", min=15000)
    .exclusion("conflict", entity="patient", relation="med", values=["A", "B"])
    .dependency("check", when={...}, then_require={...})
    .to_knowledge_base()
)

Methods: .unique(), .exclusion(), .range_rule(), .dependency(), .entity(), .to_yaml(), .to_file(), .to_knowledge_base()

docs.title: set_llm_backend(backend)
docs.text:
Swap the NL-to-Logic translator.

from axiomguard import set_llm_backend
from axiomguard.backends.anthropic_llm import anthropic_translator

set_llm_backend(anthropic_translator)

docs.title: set_entity_resolver(resolver)
docs.text: Set a custom entity resolver.

docs.title: set_knowledge_base(kb) / get_knowledge_base()
docs.text: Set/get the default KnowledgeBase.

docs.title: load_rules(path)
docs.text: Load .axiom.yml rules into the default KnowledgeBase.

docs.title: Claim
docs.text:
Pydantic model for a Subject-Relation-Object triple.

from axiomguard import Claim

claim = Claim(subject="company", relation="location", object="Bangkok", negated=False)
Field Type Description subject str Entity name relation str Relation type object str Value negated bool True if negation

docs.title: VerificationResult
docs.text:
Dataclass returned by verify().

Field Type Description is_hallucinating bool True if contradiction proved reason str Human-readable explanation confidence str "proven" or "uncertain" violated_rules list[dict] Rule metadata for violated rules contradicted_claims list[int] Indices of contradicting claims

docs.title: CorrectionResult
docs.text:
Dataclass returned by generate_with_guard().

Field Type Description status str "verified", "corrected", "failed", etc. response str Final response text attempts int Total attempts made history list[CorrectionAttempt] Full attempt log final_verification VerificationResult Last verification result

docs.title: KnowledgeBase
docs.text:
Rule compiler and verification engine.

Method Description load(path) Load .axiom.yml file load_string(content) Load from YAML string verify(claims, axioms) Verify claims against rules add_rule(rule) Add a compiled rule axiom_relations() Get all relations with rules run_examples() Run inline test examples rule_count Number of loaded rules constraint_count Number of Z3 constraints

docs.text: See the full CHANGELOG.md on GitHub for detailed version history.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: See the full CONTRIBUTING.md on GitHub.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/witchwasin/AxiomGuard.git
cd AxiomGuard
pip install -e ".[all,dev]"
pytest tests/

docs.title: Where to Contribute
docs.text:
• NL-to-Logic translation — entity normalization, complex sentences
  • New rule types — comparisons, temporal logic, negation
  • New LLM backends — Gemini, Mistral, Cohere
  • Benchmarks — HaluEval, TruthfulQA
  • Examples — new domain rule files and demo scripts

All ideas, feedback, and PRs are welcome.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Runnable example scripts are in the examples/ directory.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
File: examples/basic_usage.py

The simplest possible AxiomGuard demo. No API key needed.

python examples/basic_usage.py

from axiomguard import verify

axioms = ["The company is in Bangkok", "The CEO is Somchai"]

result = verify("The company is in Chiang Mai", axioms)
print(result.is_hallucinating)  # True

docs.title: Loan Approval Demo
docs.text:
File: examples/loan_approval_demo.py

Shows RuleBuilder creating rules programmatically, then verifying claims.

python examples/loan_approval_demo.py

Demonstrates:

  • Building rules with RuleBuilder (range, unique)
  • Thai entity aliases
  • Salary/age validation
  • Exporting rules to YAML

docs.title: Medical Bot Safety Guard
docs.text:
File: examples/medical_bot_guard.py + examples/medical_rules.axiom.yml

Catches dangerous medical hallucinations using YAML rules.

python examples/medical_bot_guard.py

Scenarios covered:

  • Drug interaction (Warfarin + Aspirin)
  • Dosage violation (Paracetamol > 4000mg)
  • Blood type contradiction
  • Allergy cross-reactivity (Penicillin → Amoxicillin)

docs.title: Document to Rules Demo (Legacy)
docs.text:
File: examples/document_to_rules_demo.py

Deprecated

This demo uses generate_rules() which is deprecated. AxiomGuard follows the BYOR (Bring Your Own Rules) philosophy — domain experts write rules, not LLMs. See Why We Don't Auto-Generate Rules.

Shows programmatic rule creation with RuleBuilder.

python examples/document_to_rules_demo.py

docs.title: Real LLM Usage
docs.text:
File: examples/real_llm_usage.py

Full pipeline with Anthropic Claude API.

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
python examples/real_llm_usage.py

docs.title: YAML Rule Files
docs.text: File Domain Rules examples/loan_rules.axiom.yml Personal loan (Thai) Salary floor, employment minimum examples/medical_rules.axiom.yml Medical safety Drug interactions, dosage limits, blood type

docs.title: Do I Need an API Key?
docs.text:
Not always. It depends on what you use:

Feature API Key Required? verify() with simple sentences No (mock backend) verify_with_kb() with YAML rules No RuleBuilder (programmatic rules) No generate_with_guard() (self-correction) Yes Real LLM extraction (complex sentences) Yes

docs.title: Setting Up
docs.text:
Anthropic (Claude) — RecommendedOpenAI (GPT-4o)Local LLM (Ollama) 
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-api03-..."

Get your key at console.anthropic.com

export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

Get your key at platform.openai.com

No API key needed. Just install and run Ollama:

# Install Ollama
curl -fsSL https://ollama.ai/install.sh | sh

# Pull a model
ollama pull llama3.1

# Use in AxiomGuard

from axiomguard.backends.generic_http_llm import create_http_translator
import axiomguard

axiomguard.set_llm_backend(create_http_translator(model="llama3.1"))

docs.title: Persistent Setup
docs.text:
Add to your shell profile (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc):

# ~/.zshrc
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-api03-..."

Then reload: source ~/.zshrc

Each user provides their own key

No API keys are bundled with the package. Each user sets up their own key and pays for their own usage.

docs.title: Basic Install
docs.text:
pip install axiomguard

This installs the core package with Z3 verification. Works immediately with the built-in mock backend — no API key needed.

docs.title: With LLM Backends
docs.text:
For production use with real LLM-powered extraction:

Anthropic (Claude)OpenAI (GPT-4o)Everything 
pip install "axiomguard[anthropic]"

pip install "axiomguard[openai]"

pip install "axiomguard[all]"

Includes: Anthropic, OpenAI, ChromaDB, Qdrant

docs.title: From Source (Development)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/witchwasin/AxiomGuard.git
cd AxiomGuard
pip install -e ".[all,dev]"
pytest tests/

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python: 3.9+
  • Z3 Solver: Installed automatically via z3-solver pip package
  • OS: Linux, macOS, Windows

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
import axiomguard
print(axiomguard.__version__)  # 0.5.0

docs.title: 1. Verify a Response (3 lines)
docs.text:
from axiomguard import verify

result = verify("The company is in Chiang Mai", ["The company is in Bangkok"])
print(result.is_hallucinating)  # True
print(result.reason)            # Z3 proved contradiction (UNSAT): ...

No API key needed — the built-in mock backend handles simple sentences.

docs.title: 2. YAML Rules + Knowledge Base
docs.text:
Create company.axiom.yml:

axiomguard: "0.3"
domain: company_facts

entities:
  - name: company
    aliases: ["firm", "org"]

rules:
  - name: hq_location
    type: unique
    entity: company
    relation: location
    value: Bangkok
    severity: error
    message: "HQ is Bangkok — not negotiable."

  - name: ceo_identity
    type: unique
    entity: company
    relation: ceo
    value: Somchai
    severity: error
    message: "CEO is Somchai."

from axiomguard import KnowledgeBase, verify_with_kb

kb = KnowledgeBase()
kb.load("company.axiom.yml")

result = verify_with_kb("The CEO is John", kb)
print(result.is_hallucinating)   # True
print(result.violated_rules)     # [ceo_identity]

docs.title: 3. Self-Correction Loop
docs.text:
Auto-detect and fix hallucinations:

from axiomguard import KnowledgeBase, generate_with_guard

kb = KnowledgeBase()
kb.load("company.axiom.yml")

result = generate_with_guard(
    prompt="Tell me about the company",
    kb=kb,
    llm_generate=my_llm_function,  # any (str) -> str callable
    max_retries=2,
)

print(result.status)    # "verified" | "corrected" | "failed"
print(result.response)  # The verified response
print(result.attempts)  # How many tries

88% fix rate

The self-correction loop fixes 88% of hallucinations within 2 retries at ~$0.02 per correction (using Haiku).

docs.title: 4. Inspect Extracted Claims (Audit Trail)
docs.text:
AxiomGuard always exposes what the LLM extracted before Z3 processes it:

from axiomguard import extract_claims

claims = extract_claims("Transfer 800,000 THB to a crypto wallet")
for claim in claims:
    print(f"  {claim.subject}.{claim.relation} = {claim.object}")

# Auditor can verify: Did the LLM extract correctly?
# If it extracted "8000" instead of "800000" — that's an EXTRACTION error,
# not a Z3 error. Transparent. Traceable.

BYOR — Bring Your Own Rules

AxiomGuard is an enforcement engine. Domain experts write the rules in YAML, AxiomGuard enforces them with Z3 mathematical proof. See Architecture Philosophy.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• YAML Rules Format — learn all rule types
  • LLM Backends — connect Claude, GPT, or local models
  • API Reference — full function documentation

docs.title: Why We Don't Auto-Generate Rules
docs.text:
Architectural Decision

AxiomGuard deliberately does NOT use LLMs to generate YAML rules from documents. This page explains why.

docs.title: The Problem with AI-Generated Rules
docs.text:
Using an LLM to read a policy document and output YAML rules sounds efficient. In practice, it creates:

  1. Fake citations — LLMs invent constraints that don't exist in the source document
  2. Hallucinated thresholds — "Maximum 10 items" when the policy says 5
  3. Liability gap — No human signed off on the generated rules
  4. False confidence — Teams trust rules they didn't write or review

docs.title: Our Approach: BYOR (Bring Your Own Rules)
docs.text:
AxiomGuard is an enforcement engine, not a rule generator.

We DO We DON'T Enforce YAML rules via Z3 Auto-generate rules from documents Validate rule syntax Interpret legal/medical PDFs Provide visual rule editor (Axiom Studio, coming in v0.7.0) Claim our rules are "correct" Return pass/fail with hardcoded messages Provide legal/medical advice

docs.title: How to Write Rules
docs.text:
• YAML Rules Format — learn all rule types
  • Programmatic Rules — build rules from code with RuleBuilder
  • Axiom Studio (v0.7.0) — visual UI to help humans write and test YAML

docs.title: Legacy Note
docs.text: The generate_rules() function exists in the codebase from v0.5.x but is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Do not use it for production workloads.

docs.title: Architecture Philosophy
docs.text:
See the full Architecture Philosophy document on GitHub.

AxiomGuard is not smart. It is correct.

We follow the "Dumb but Unbreakable" doctrine — deterministic, auditable, zero-surprise enforcement.

docs.title: LLM Backends
docs.text: AxiomGuard is provider-agnostic. The Z3 verification engine is always the same — only the NL-to-Logic translator changes. Swap backends in one line.

docs.title: Available Backends
docs.text: Backend Package API Key Quality Mock (built-in) None None Simple sentences only Anthropic (Claude) axiomguard[anthropic] ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Excellent OpenAI (GPT-4o) axiomguard[openai] OPENAI_API_KEY Excellent Local (Ollama/vLLM) None None Good (model-dependent) Custom None Varies You decide

docs.title: Anthropic (Claude)
docs.text:
pip install "axiomguard[anthropic]"
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."

import axiomguard
from axiomguard.backends.anthropic_llm import anthropic_translator

axiomguard.set_llm_backend(anthropic_translator)

Specify a model:

from axiomguard.backends.anthropic_llm import create_anthropic_translator

backend = create_anthropic_translator(model="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514")
axiomguard.set_llm_backend(backend)

docs.title: OpenAI (GPT-4o)
docs.text:
pip install "axiomguard[openai]"
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

import axiomguard
from axiomguard.backends.openai_llm import openai_translator

axiomguard.set_llm_backend(openai_translator)

docs.title: Local LLMs (Ollama / vLLM)
docs.text:
No API key needed. Works with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint:

from axiomguard.backends.generic_http_llm import create_http_translator

# Ollama (default: http://localhost:11434/v1)
axiomguard.set_llm_backend(create_http_translator(model="llama3.1"))

# vLLM / custom endpoint
axiomguard.set_llm_backend(create_http_translator(
    base_url="http://my-server:8080/v1",
    model="mistral-7b",
    api_key="optional-key",
))

docs.title: Custom Backend
docs.text:
Any function (str) -> dict works:

import axiomguard

def my_backend(text: str) -> dict:
    # Your logic — call any API, model, or rule engine
    return {"subject": "company", "relation": "location", "object": "Bangkok"}

axiomguard.set_llm_backend(my_backend)

docs.title: Programmatic Rules (RuleBuilder)
docs.text: Build rules dynamically from code, databases, or APIs using the fluent RuleBuilder API.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from axiomguard import RuleBuilder

builder = RuleBuilder(domain="hr_policy")

builder.unique("one_department", entity="employee", relation="department")
builder.range_rule("age_limit", entity="employee", relation="age", min=18, max=65)

kb = builder.to_knowledge_base()

docs.title: Fluent Chaining
docs.text:
All methods return self for chaining:

kb = (
    RuleBuilder(domain="loan")
    .entity("applicant", aliases=["ผู้กู้", "ลูกค้า"])
    .unique("hq", entity="company", relation="location", value="Bangkok")
    .range_rule("salary", entity="applicant", relation="salary", min=15000)
    .exclusion("conflict", entity="applicant", relation="status",
               values=["approved", "blacklisted"])
    .to_knowledge_base()
)

docs.title: .unique()
docs.text:
builder.unique(
    "rule_name",
    entity="company",
    relation="location",
    value="Bangkok",          # optional: fixed value
    severity="error",         # error | warning | info
    message="HQ is Bangkok",  # human-readable explanation
)

docs.title: .exclusion()
docs.text:
builder.exclusion(
    "drug_conflict",
    entity="patient",
    relation="medication",
    values=["Warfarin", "Aspirin"],
    message="Cannot prescribe together.",
)

docs.title: .range_rule()
docs.text:
builder.range_rule(
    "age_limit",
    entity="applicant",
    relation="age",
    min=20,             # optional
    max=60,             # optional
    value_type="int",   # int | float
    message="Must be 20-60.",
)

docs.title: .dependency()
docs.text:
builder.dependency(
    "employment_check",
    when={
        "entity": "applicant",
        "relation": "employment_months",
        "value": "6",
        "value_type": "int",
        "operator": "<",
    },
    then_require={
        "relation": "status",
        "value": "rejected",
    },
    message="< 6 months employment = rejected.",
)

docs.title: .entity()
docs.text: builder.entity("applicant", aliases=["ผู้กู้", "client"], description="Loan applicant")

docs.title: To KnowledgeBase (for verification)
docs.text:
kb = builder.to_knowledge_base()
result = kb.verify(claims)

docs.title: To YAML string
docs.text:
yaml_str = builder.to_yaml()
print(yaml_str)

docs.title: To file
docs.text: builder.to_file("rules/generated.axiom.yml")

docs.title: Dynamic Rules from Database
docs.text:
from axiomguard import RuleBuilder

builder = RuleBuilder(domain="dynamic")

# Load rules from your database
for row in db.query("SELECT * FROM policies WHERE country = 'TH'"):
    builder.unique(
        row.name,
        entity=row.entity,
        relation=row.relation,
        value=row.value,
        message=row.description,
    )

# Different rules per user type
if user.is_premium:
    builder.range_rule("credit_limit", entity="user",
                       relation="credit", max=1000000)
else:
    builder.range_rule("credit_limit", entity="user",
                       relation="credit", max=100000)

kb = builder.to_knowledge_base()

docs.title: When to Use RuleBuilder vs YAML
docs.text: Use Case RuleBuilder YAML File Rules from database/API Best - Rules change per user/request Best - Static compliance rules - Best Domain expert review - Best Version-controlled policies - Best Rapid prototyping Best -

docs.title: Self-Correction Loop
docs.text: AxiomGuard v0.5.0 can automatically detect and fix hallucinations by re-prompting the LLM with Z3 proof feedback.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
User Prompt + KnowledgeBase
    │
    ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ LLM Generate │  attempt 1
└──────┬───────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ Extract Claims│  multi-claim SRO extraction
└──────┬───────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ Z3 Verify    │  rules + axiom facts
└──────┬───────┘
       │
  ┌────┴────┐
  │         │
 SAT      UNSAT
  │         │
  ▼         ▼
DONE   Build correction prompt
       (include Z3 proof trace)
              │
              ▼
         Retry (max 2)

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from axiomguard import KnowledgeBase, generate_with_guard

kb = KnowledgeBase()
kb.load("company.axiom.yml")

result = generate_with_guard(
    prompt="Tell me about the company",
    kb=kb,
    llm_generate=my_llm_function,  # (str) -> str
    max_retries=2,
)

print(result.status)     # "verified" | "corrected" | "failed"
print(result.response)   # Best available response
print(result.attempts)   # Number of attempts (1-3)

docs.title: Result Statuses
docs.text: Status Meaning "verified" Passed on first attempt — no hallucination "corrected" Had hallucinations, fixed on retry "failed" All retries exhausted, still has issues "unverifiable" Could not extract claims from response "constraint_conflict" Same error every attempt — rules may conflict

docs.title: Inspecting Attempts
docs.text:
for attempt in result.history:
    print(f"Attempt {attempt.attempt_number}:")
    print(f"  Response: {attempt.response[:100]}...")
    print(f"  Claims: {len(attempt.claims)}")
    print(f"  Hallucinating: {attempt.verification.is_hallucinating}")
    if attempt.correction_prompt:
        print(f"  Correction: {attempt.correction_prompt[:100]}...")

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
Metric Value Fix rate after 1 retry ~70% Fix rate after 2 retries ~88% Average cost per correction ~$0.02 (Haiku) 3rd retry added fix rate Only +3%

Why max_retries=2?

Diminishing returns: 3rd retry adds only 3% fix rate for +$0.01 cost. The default of 2 is the sweet spot.

docs.title: Three-Layer Fail-Safe
docs.text:
1. max_retries — Stop after N correction attempts
  2. timeout_seconds — Total time budget for all attempts
  3. max_tokens_budget — Optional token cost cap

docs.title: YAML Rules Format
docs.text: AxiomGuard uses .axiom.yml files to define domain rules. These files are designed to be readable by non-programmers — lawyers, doctors, compliance officers can review and edit them directly.

docs.title: File Structure
docs.text:
axiomguard: "0.3"          # Format version
domain: company_facts       # Domain identifier

entities:                    # Entity definitions (optional)
  - name: company
    aliases: ["firm", "org", "บริษัท"]

rules:                       # One or more rules
  - name: rule_name
    type: unique|exclusion|dependency|range
    ...

docs.title: unique — Single Value
docs.text:
An entity can only have one value for this relation. The most common rule type.

- name: one_headquarters
  type: unique
  entity: company
  relation: location
  value: Bangkok
  severity: error
  message: "Company has exactly one HQ location."

Z3 logic: ForAll(s, o1, o2): Rel(r, s, o1) AND Rel(r, s, o2) → o1 = o2

Use cases: HQ location, CEO name, blood type, primary doctor

docs.title: exclusion — Mutual Conflict
docs.text:
These specific values cannot coexist for the same entity.

- name: warfarin_aspirin
  type: exclusion
  entity: patient
  relation: medication
  values: [Warfarin, Aspirin]
  severity: error
  message: "Warfarin and Aspirin cannot be prescribed together."

Z3 logic: ForAll(s): NOT(Rel(r, s, "Warfarin") AND Rel(r, s, "Aspirin"))

Use cases: Drug interactions, conflicting statuses, mutually exclusive options

docs.title: dependency — If-Then
docs.text:
If condition A is true, then B must also be true.

- name: min_employment
  type: dependency
  when:
    entity: applicant
    relation: employment_months
    value: "6"
    value_type: int
    operator: "<"
  then:
    require:
      relation: approval_status
      value: rejected
  severity: error
  message: "Applicants with < 6 months employment must be rejected."

Supported operators: =, !=, >, <, >=, <=

Supported value types: string, int, float, date

Use cases: Conditional approval, policy enforcement, eligibility checks

docs.title: range — Numeric Bounds
docs.text:
A numeric attribute must be within min/max bounds.

- name: age_limit
  type: range
  entity: applicant
  relation: age
  value_type: int
  min: 20
  max: 60
  severity: error
  message: "Applicant must be 20-60 years old."

You can specify min only, max only, or both.

Use cases: Age limits, salary floors, dosage caps, date ranges

docs.title: Entities & Aliases
docs.text:
Define entities so AxiomGuard resolves aliases automatically:

entities:
  - name: applicant
    aliases: ["ผู้กู้", "ลูกค้า", "ผู้สมัคร", "client"]
    description: "Loan applicant"

  - name: company
    aliases: ["firm", "org", "บริษัท"]

When the LLM extracts "ผู้กู้" as a subject, AxiomGuard resolves it to "applicant" before Z3 verification.

docs.title: Severity Levels
docs.text:
severity: error    # Hard violation — hallucination detected
severity: warning  # Soft violation — flagged but not blocked
severity: info     # Informational only

docs.title: Inline Examples (Self-Testing Rules)
docs.text:
Embed test cases directly in your rules:

- name: hq_location
  type: unique
  entity: company
  relation: location
  value: Bangkok
  examples:
    - input: "The company is in Bangkok"
      axioms: ["The company is in Bangkok"]
      expect: pass
    - input: "The company is in Chiang Mai"
      axioms: ["The company is in Bangkok"]
      expect: fail

Run tests: kb.run_examples() returns (passed, total, failures).

docs.title: Full Example
docs.text:
axiomguard: "0.3"
domain: personal_loan

entities:
  - name: applicant
    aliases: ["ผู้กู้", "ลูกค้า", "ผู้สมัคร"]

rules:
  - name: age_limit
    type: range
    entity: applicant
    relation: age
    value_type: int
    min: 20
    max: 60
    severity: error
    message: "ผู้กู้ต้องมีอายุ 20-60 ปี"

  - name: min_salary
    type: range
    entity: applicant
    relation: salary_thb
    value_type: int
    min: 15000
    severity: error
    message: "เงินเดือนขั้นต่ำ 15,000 บาท"

  - name: one_approval_status
    type: unique
    entity: applicant
    relation: approval_status
    severity: error
    message: "สถานะอนุมัติมีได้เพียงค่าเดียว"

  - name: drug_interaction
    type: exclusion
    entity: applicant
    relation: insurance_type
    values: [health, life_critical]
    severity: warning
    message: "Cannot combine health and life-critical insurance."

  - name: probation_check
    type: dependency
    when:
      entity: applicant
      relation: employment_months
      value: "6"
      value_type: int
      operator: "<"
    then:
      require:
        relation: approval_status
        value: rejected
    severity: error
    message: "อายุงานต่ำกว่า 6 เดือนต้องปฏิเสธ"
